name: Codecov API
what_it_does: Codecov's API for accessing code coverage reports, coverage trends, and diff coverage data across repositories integrated with CI/CD pipelines.
use_cases: Fetching coverage percentages for pull request coverage gates
Querying historical coverage trends for engineering dashboards
Comparing coverage between branches or commits
Building custom coverage reporting into team portals
Identifying components with decreasing or insufficient coverage
not_for: Running tests or generating coverage data (requires test runner integration)
Code quality analysis beyond coverage metrics
Teams not using Codecov for coverage reporting
best_when: You use Codecov for code coverage tracking and need to query or display coverage data in custom tools or dashboards.
avoid_when: You don't use Codecov, or need code quality analysis beyond coverage percentages.

auth.notes: API tokens for authentication. Tokens generated per user in Codecov settings. Passed as Authorization header. Token provides access to all repos the user has access to.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for public repos; free for up to 5 users on private repos
pricing.notes: Free for open-source. Paid plans for private repositories above 5 users. Enterprise with SSO and compliance features. Sentry acquired Codecov in 2023.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error responses with JSON messages. Error detail is adequate but not exceptional.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All primary API endpoints are read-only — fully idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Coverage upload (CI integration) and API query are separate concerns — API only reads, never uploads
Repository identified by owner/repo slug — must know exact naming including organization
Coverage data may lag after CI runs — poll for report readiness before querying
Component coverage (file, directory level) requires navigating nested API responses
Codecov was acquired by Sentry — some product direction may shift; verify documentation currency

security_score_components.security_notes: Code coverage platform. Token auth. Coverage reports may reveal untested code paths — security-sensitive info. Self-hosted Codecov available for private repos.
